dbbot v0.8.0-beta.3 adds default-on post-deploy acceptance checks for the MySQL Ansible entry playbooks and keeps them explicitly disableable for controlled troubleshooting.

This beta introduces a shared `post_tasks` dispatcher and reusable validation profiles for MySQL instance health, asynchronous replication, Group Replication, InnoDB Cluster, MySQL Router, Router HA, MHA-Go, classic MHA, keepalived-based master-slave HA, exporter services, and unsafe uninstall cleanup.

The new checks make deployment success stricter: playbooks now verify runtime service state, listening ports, SQL connectivity, replication health, zero-lag master-replica catch-up by default, cluster member state, Router and VIP placement, exporter `/metrics` endpoints, and uninstall cleanup where applicable. Operators can temporarily skip these checks with `fcs_run_post_deploy_checks=false`.

This beta also hardens post-check handling for sensitive MySQL credentials by hiding password-bearing validation tasks from Ansible output, and updates the MySQL documentation to explain the new acceptance behavior and switch.

Backup and restore entry playbooks are intentionally not included in this post-deploy acceptance scope yet.
